speaker: Petr Pavel
text: Populism shouldn't be labeled as inherently bad — listening to citizens' frustration and translating it into policy is what politics is for; the line is crossed only when populism turns against the institutions that protect democracy.
context: Noted that some politicians proudly accept the populist label.

grounded_formulations.voice_name: Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
